comments.text: LAKE MANCHESTER and TV TOWERS: VFR waypoints (on Sweden)
we never use square brackets or descriptive tags, only <best guess in angle brackets>
<you> uh <go> is unclear to be fair
the word 'level' should not be in first segment
good job finding the helipad :)
(location used as VFR reporting point)
the word ARCHER is missing after PIPER
the flight can be found using ADSB and partially audible words that can be confirmed through research are to be transcribed as audible

any input here automatically selects the DR, typing - > without the space converts into an arrow in the pdf as well, added 'select all' checkbox in top right, PDF will count how many DRs were flagged Major and show avg % changed across the chosen DRs, copy button next to GK

couldn't find the callsign at the end of the first segment but it sounds like a readback from Pilot
traffic has to be reported 'in sight'
the first word doesn't sound like SWISS, maybe "and"
word 'climb' is also missing before 'flight level'
timestamps adjusted
helo -> djdj
FALSE CAPE is VFR reporting point and should be all caps
'the' should also be deleted before 'approach'
caps, facility name
alright should be lowercase (and i hear hello, not alright)

we never use square brackets or descriptive tags, only <best guess in angle brackets>
the first word of the callsign is not unintelligible nor should it be left out as the reviewer did, it's REX
<BUGSU> is only in brackets because of the word before it being unclear and blending into it
POSTMAN, very easily verified
reviewer incorrectly changed role to Pilot, it is ATC but other corrections are valid
the 2nd transmission is not in reply to the 1st
this could be either readback from pilot or instruction from ATC, so has to be unknown role.
it's 'decimal one' but should have been skipped
this could have been skipped instead of submitted with a transcript that barely matches the audio

--> departure, goedenavond ALPINE SEVEN EIGHT ROMEO ZULU ... BERGI FIVE VICTOR.
Non-English
This is Dutch
Five --> five
--> HELISERVICE WHISKEY ALFA TWO flying back to Emden, ETA Emden minutes five five, estimating <KUVEK> minutes three six ... QNH one zero two eight (no caps)
filler words are also important to transcribe
first two words possible non- English
WOODY two Sierra --> BERGI TWO SIERRA
Sandcat, Skyvector etc can confirm facility names based on GK
last speaker is 'unknown' because speaker 4 was kept and speaker 3 deleted when deleting duplicate segments
RXA is the ICAO, you need callsign extension to see it converted to the telephony REX
this could be ATC or a pilot readback so role is unknown
needs to be
the last waypoint is actually GEDSO
